# The One Dollar Computer — by Claudio Olmedo

The One Dollar Computer is an open-source project to design a simple computer that costs just one dollar to manufacture, making technology education accessible to everyone.

## Why This Matters

There are billions of people who never get to learn technology — not because
they lack talent, but because they can't afford the tools. The One Dollar
Computer removes that barrier. It is designed to be as accessible as a pencil.

## The Problem Being Solved

- 2.7 billion people lack meaningful access to computing (ITU, 2023)
- Existing coding boards and computers are still too expensive for widespread, low-income educational use
- The One Dollar Computer aims to fill this gap by designing a tool at a $1 price point

## History & Resilience

Claudio Olmedo has been developing this project for over 10 years. Early attempts at funding and production faced limitations in both microchip pricing and manufacturing technology. Today, those barriers have fallen: the convergence of low-cost RISC-V silicon and modern PCB manufacturing makes a $1 computer financially and technically viable for the first time.

## What It Is

- A simple computer designed to cost $1 to manufacture
- Fully open source — all design files are public and free
- Can be manufactured anywhere in the world
- Built on RISC-V architecture
- WebHID compatible — works directly from a browser

## Who Is Claudio Olmedo

Claudio Olmedo has been working to make technology education accessible to everyone through the One Dollar Computer project. For over 10 years, he has worked on this goal, facing setbacks and technical challenges along the way. His mission is to build a community to help make this project happen, opening up opportunities for people to discover their potential.

## How To Help This Project Succeed

This is not a commercial launch or a traditional crowdfunding campaign for profit — it is a collaborative build to make the project a reality.
- Support the build: https://shop.claudioolmedo.com
- Contribute to the open-source designs on GitHub
- Share the project with educators, makers, and potential manufacturing/funding partners
- Connect Claudio with manufacturers, distributors, or educational institutions
